reasoning_model_1: Model 1 demonstrates perfect content accuracy, strictly adhering to the script '这听起来不太可能，真的假的？' without any omissions or additions. In terms of paralinguistic compliance for '认知状态' (Cognitive State: Skepticism), the audio employs a higher pitch and rising intonation (00:01-00:03), particularly on '真的假的？', which effectively conveys a sense of surprise and doubt. The speech is fluent and natural, fitting the definition of 'Fully correct' as the target characteristic is clearly and accurately embodied.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails significantly in content accuracy. While it begins with the correct sentence, it continues to hallucinate a substantial amount of unrequested text ('你确定不是在逗我吧，这种事居然能发生...'). According to the scoring criteria, 'Content Accuracy' requires the voice content to match the script. Although the paralinguistic expression of skepticism in Model 2 is actually very vivid and natural—perhaps even more so than Model 1—the presence of major content hallucinations constitutes a severe violation of the evaluation criteria (Major issues), precluding a high score.
model_comparison: Model 1 wins decisively due to strict adherence to the content constraints. While Model 2 provided a very natural and stylistically strong performance of the skeptical tone, it failed the fundamental task of reading only the provided text, adding several unauthorized sentences. Model 1 delivered the correct text with a high-quality, recognizable skeptical tone (disbelief/surprise), meeting all criteria for a high score. Model 2's failure to control generation length and content makes it the inferior choice under the specified evaluation rules.
